summary: Hotz delivers a scathing critique of people who chase external markers of success—high compensation, titles, prestigious credentials—rather than developing actual skills. He mimics the voice of someone obsessed with status symbols before dismissing them as losers who wasted their lives pursuing things 'made up by other men.' The post targets tech workers who optimized for career advancement metrics rather than genuine capability, and who now find themselves laid off despite their prestigious backgrounds.
key_points: External status markers (TC, titles, credentials, reports) are hollow achievements
Prestigious schools and compensation packages don't equal real value or importance
Chasing status instead of skill leads to vulnerability when layoffs come
The tech industry rewards people for playing status games rather than building real capabilities
True worth comes from skill, not from symbols created by others
money_quotes: I have big package. I am important.
You can see I am important? You can see I have big package?
you got laid off from an ad company after spending half your life chasing stuff made up by other men
you sought status instead of skill
you are a loser. get the fuck out
key_insight: Optimizing for status symbols and career metrics instead of genuine skill leaves you with nothing when the music stops.
